title: linanthus androsaceus subsp. micranthus
intro: Linanthus androsaceus subsp. micranthus, also known as the dwarf rockjasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linanthus androsaceus subsp. micranthus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for topical applications. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns.
